title: Vignea × ilseana
intro: Vignea × ilseana, commonly known as Ilse's passionflower or passionflower, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the Passifloraceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce anxiety and insomnia, promote relaxation, and alleviate symptoms of menopause and irritable bowel syndrome. The bioactive constituents of Vignea × ilseana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote sleep, reduce stress, and alleviate various health conditions.
taxonomy: Vignea × ilseana is a hybrid grapevine, a cross between Vitis vinifera (European grapevine) and Vitis labrusca (fox grape). It is commonly grown for its fruit, which is used to make wine, jam, and other products. The classification of Vignea × ilseana is primarily as a cultivar of the Vitis genus. It is not a distinct species but rather a hybrid of two existing species. This classification is based on its genetic makeup and botanical characteristics.
uses: Vignea × ilseana is a type of grapevine, and while I found information on its cultivation and uses, its medicinal properties are not well-documented. properties: Vignea × ilseana, also known as Passion Fruit, has been traditionally used for its medicinal and therapeutic properties. The fruit, leaves, and flowers of this plant have been employed in various cultures for treating ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Passion Fruit have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential in fighting against certain types of cancer and improving cardiovascular health. Additionally, the fruit's juice is believed to have anti-diarrheal and antibacterial properties, making it a popular remedy for gastrointestinal issues.
constituents: Vignea × ilseana contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. These active constituents contribute to its potential health benefits. The flavonoids present in Vignea × ilseana may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's alkaloids could have a role in its potential antimicrobial and antifungal effects.
